rag_rules: Place input assumptions in component type annexes when bounds are concrete or visible
Use assign only for implementation-level bindings with existing output features or auxiliary variables
Avoid subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants
generic_rules: Use named constants or visible relations for concrete bounds in assumptions and guarantees
Align helper declaration types with expression types to avoid type mismatches
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use `eq` for equation declarations and `assign` for implementation-level bindings
Use `guarantee` for implementation-level guarantees and `assume` for assumptions
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not encode AADL property names as AGREE variables without explicit typed ports or constants
